title: Anti-sars-cov-2 spike protein antibodies and methods of use
abstract: The invention provides anti-SARS-CoV-2 spike (S) protein antibodies and methods of using the same.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. An isolated antibody that binds SARS-CoV-2 spike (S) protein, wherein the antibody binds to an epitope between amino acid residues 439-541 of SARS-CoV-2 S protein (SEQ ID NO: 1). 2. The antibody of claim 1 , wherein the antibody binds to an epitope between amino acid residues 439-498 of SARS-CoV-2 S protein (SEQ ID NO: 1). 3. The antibody of claim 1 or 2, wherein the antibody binds to an epitope comprising at least one of amino acid residues Y449, Y453, F456, A475, Y489, and Q493 of SARS-CoV-2 S protein (SEQ ID NO: 1). 4. The antibody of claim 3, wherein the antibody binds to an epitope comprising the amino acid residues Y449, Y453, F456, A475, Y489, and Q493 of SARS-CoV-2 S protein (SEQ ID NO: 1). 5. An isolated antibody that binds SARS-CoV-2 S protein, wherein the antibody comprises the following complementary determining regions (CDRs): (a) a CDR-H1 comprising the amino acid sequence of GFSFSSYGMH (SEQ ID NO: 2); (b) a CDR-H2 comprising the amino acid sequence of WYDGSDK (SEQ ID NO: 3); (c) a CDR-H3 comprising the amino acid sequence of ARERYFDWIFDF (SEQ ID NO: 4); (d) a CDR-L1 comprising the amino acid sequence of RASQSVSSSYLA (SEQ ID NO: 5); (e) a CDR-L2 comprising the amino acid sequence of GASSRAT (SEQ ID NO:6); and (f) a CDR-L3 comprising the amino acid sequence of QQYGSSWT (SEQ ID NO: 7). 6. The antibody of claim 5, wherein the antibody further comprises the following heavy chain framework regions (FRs): (a) an FR-H1 comprising the amino acid sequence of QVQLVESGGGVVQPGRSLRLSCAAS (SEQ ID NO: 8); (b) an FR-H2 comprising the amino acid sequence of WVRQAPGKGLEWVAVI (SEQ ID NO: 9); (c) an FR-H3 comprising the amino acid sequence of YYADSVKGRFTISRDNSKNTLYLQLNSLRAEDTAIYYC (SEQ ID NO: 10); and (d) an FR-H4 comprising the amino acid sequence of WGQGTLVTVSS (SEQ ID NO: 11). 7. The antibody of claim 5, wherein the antibody further comprises the following light chain FRs: (a) an FR-L1 comprising the amino acid sequence of EIVLTQSPGTLSLSPGERATLSC (SEQ ID NO: 12); (b) an FR-L2 comprising the amino acid sequence of WYQQKPGQAPRLLIY (SEQ ID NO: 13); (c) an FR-L3 comprising the amino acid sequence of GIPDRFSGSGSGTDFTLTISRLEPEDFAVYYC (SEQ ID NO: 14); and (d) an FR-L4 comprising the amino acid sequence of FGQGTKVEIK (SEQ ID NO: 15). 8. The antibody of any one of claims 5-7, wherein the antibody comprises a heavy chain variable (VH) domain comprising an amino acid sequence having at least 95% sequence identity to the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 16 and a light chain variable (VL) domain comprising an amino acid sequence having at least 95% sequence identity to the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 17. 9. The antibody of any one of claims 5-8, wherein the antibody comprises a VH domain comprising the amino acid sequence having at least 96% sequence identity to the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 16 and a light chain domain comprising the amino acid sequence having at least 96% sequence identity to the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 17. 10. The antibody of any one of claims 5-9, wherein the antibody comprises a VH domain comprising the amino acid sequence having at least 97% sequence identity to the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 16 and a VL domain comprising the amino acid sequence having at least 97% sequence identity to the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 17. 11. The antibody of any one of claims 5-10, wherein the antibody comprises a VH domain comprising the amino acid sequence having at least 98% sequence identity to the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 16 and a VL domain comprising the amino acid sequence having at least 98% sequence identity to the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 17. 12. The antibody of any one of claims 5-11 , wherein the antibody comprises a VH domain comprising the amino acid sequence having at least 99% sequence identity to the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 16 and a VL domain comprising the amino acid sequence having at least 99% sequence identity to the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 17. 13. An isolated antibody that binds SARS-CoV-2 S protein, wherein the antibody comprises a VH domain comprising the amino acid of SEQ ID NO: 16 and a VL domain comprising the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 17. 14. The antibody of any one of claims 1-13, wherein the antibody binds SARS-CoV S protein (SEQ ID NO: 18). 15. The antibody of claim 14, wherein the antibody binds to an epitope between amino acids residues 270-510 of SARS-CoV S protein (SEQ ID NO: 18). 16. The antibody of any one of claims 1-15, wherein the antibody is capable of inhibiting binding of SARS-CoV-2 S protein to angiotensin-converting enzyme 2 (ACE2) receptor. 17. The antibody of claim 16, wherein the antibody is capable of inhibiting binding of SARS-CoV- 2 S protein to ACE2 receptor by at least about 80% at a concentration of about 330 nM or with an ECso of about 40 nM, as determined by an in vitro receptor binding inhibition assay. 18. The antibody of any one of claims 1-17, wherein the antibody is capable of neutralizing SARS-CoV-2. 19. The antibody of claim 18, wherein the antibody is capable of neutralizing SARS-CoV-2 with an EC50 of about 5 pg/mL as determined by a plaque reduction neutralization test (PRNT). 20. The antibody of any one of claims 1-19, wherein the antibody is capable of neutralizing SARS-CoV. 21. The antibody of any one of claims 1-20, wherein the antibody binds SARS-CoV-2 S protein with a KD of between about 100 pM and about 100 nM. 22. The antibody of claim 21 , wherein the antibody binds SARS-CoV-2 S protein with a KD of between about 100 pM and about 50 nM. 23. The antibody of claim 22, wherein the antibody binds SARS-CoV-2 S protein with a KD of between about 200 pM and about 20 nM. 24. The antibody of claim 23, wherein the antibody binds SARS-CoV-2 S protein with a KD of about 300 pM or about 13 nM. 25. The antibody of any one of claims 1-24, wherein the antibody binds SARS-CoV S protein with a KD of between about 10 pM and about 10 nM. 26. The antibody of claim 25, wherein the antibody binds SARS-CoV S protein with a K D of between about 100 pM and about 10 nM. 27. The antibody of claim 26, wherein the antibody binds SARS-CoV S protein with a KD of between about 500 pM and about 2 nM. 28. The antibody of claim 27, wherein the antibody binds SARS-CoV S protein with a K D of about 1.3 nM or about 1.4 nM. 29. The antibody of any one of claims 21-28, wherein the K D is measured by a surface plasmon resonance assay at 25Â°C. 30. The anybody of any one of claims 1-29, wherein the antibody is a monoclonal antibody. 31. An isolated monoclonal antibody that binds SARS-CoV-2 S protein, wherein the antibody competes for binding to SARS-CoV-2 S protein with the antibody of any one of claims 1-30. 32. The antibody of any one of claims 1-31 , wherein the antibody is a human antibody. 33. The antibody of any one of claims 1-32, wherein the antibody is an IgG class antibody. 34. The antibody of claim 33, wherein the IgG class antibody is an lgG1 subclass antibody. 35. The antibody of any one of claims 1-32, wherein the antibody is an IgA class antibody. 36. The antibody of claim 35, wherein the IgA class antibody is an lgA1 subclass antibody. 37. The antibody of claim 35, wherein the IgA class antibody is an lgA2 subclass antibody. 38. The antibody of any one of claims 35-37, wherein the IgA class antibody is a secretory IgA (SlgA). 39. The antibody of any one of claims 1-37, wherein the antibody is a full-length antibody. 40. The antibody of any one of claims 1 -37, wherein the antibody is an antibody fragment that binds SARS-CoV-2 S protein selected from the group consisting of Fab, Fabâ, Fabâ-SH, Fv, single chain variable fragment (scFv), and (Fabâ) 2 fragments. 41. An isolated nucleic acid encoding the antibody of any one of claims 1-40. 42. A vector comprising the nucleic acid of claim 41 . 43. A host cell comprising the vector of claim 42. 44. The host cell of claim 43, wherein the host cell is a mammalian cell. 45. The host cell of claim 44, wherein the mammalian cell is a Chinese hamster ovary (CHO) cell. 46. The host cell of claim 43, wherein the host cell is a prokaryotic cell. 47. The host cell of claim 46, wherein the prokaryotic cell is an E. coli cell. 48. A method of producing the antibody of any one of claims 1-40, the method comprising culturing a host cell comprising the nucleic acid of claim 41 in a culture medium. 49. The method of claim 48, wherein the method further comprises recovering the antibody from the host cell or the culture medium. 50. A composition comprising the antibody of any one of claims 1-40. 51. A pharmaceutical composition comprising the antibody of any one of claims 1-40. 52. The pharmaceutical composition of claim 51 , further comprising a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier, excipient, or diluent. 53. The pharmaceutical composition of claim 51 or 52, wherein the pharmaceutical composition is formulated for treating a betacoronavirus infection in a subject. 54. A method of treating a subject having a betacoronavirus infection or presumed to have a betacoronavirus infection, the method comprising administering to the subject an effective amount of the antibody of any one of claims 1-40 or the pharmaceutical composition of any one of claims 51-53, thereby treating the subject. 55. A method of treating a subject at risk of having a betacoronavirus infection, the method comprising administering to the subject an effective amount of the antibody of any one of claims 1-40 or the pharmaceutical composition of any one of claims 51-53, thereby treating the subject. 56. The method of claim 54 or 55, wherein the betacoronavirus infection is with a lineage B betacoronavirus or a lineage C betacoronavirus. 57. The method of claim 56, wherein the betacoronavirus infection is with a lineage B betacoronavirus. 58. The method of claim 57, wherein the lineage B betacoronavirus is SARS-CoV-2 or SARS- CoV. 59. The method of claim 58, wherein the lineage B betacoronavirus is SARS-CoV-2. 60. The method of claim 58, wherein the lineage B betacoronavirus is SARS-CoV. 61. The method of any one of claims 54 and 56-59, wherein the subject has or is presumed to have coronavirus disease 19 (COVID-19). 62. The method of any one of claims 54, 56-58, and 60, wherein the subject has or is presumed to have severe acute respiratory syndrome (SARS). 63. The method of any one of claims 54-62, wherein the antibody is administered to the subject at a dosage of about 0.1 mg/kg to about 100 mg/kg. 64. The method of claim 63, wherein the antibody is administered to the subject at a dosage of about 1 mg/kg to about 80 mg/kg. 65. The method of claim 64, wherein the antibody is administered to the subject at a dosage of about 1 mg/kg to about 40 mg/kg. 66. The method of any one of claims 54-65, wherein the antibody is administered to the subject intravenously. 67. The method of any one of claims 54-65, wherein the antibody is administered to the subject intranasally. 68. The method of any one of claims 54-65, wherein the antibody is administered to the subject by inhalation. 69. The method of any one of claims 54-68, wherein the antibody is administered to the subject as a monotherapy. 70. The method of any one of claims 54-68, wherein the antibody is administered to the subject as a combination therapy. 71 . The method of claim 70, wherein the combination therapy comprises administering to the subject one or more additional therapeutic agents. 72. The method of claim 71 , wherein the antibody is administered to the subject prior to, concurrently with, or after administration of the one or more additional therapeutic agents. 73. The method of claim 71 or 72, wherein the one or more additional therapeutic agents comprise a second therapeutic antibody, an antifungal agent, an antiviral agent, an antiparasitic agent, an antibacterial agent, or a combination thereof. 74. The method of claim 73, wherein the one or more additional therapeutic agents comprise an antiviral agent. 75. The method of claim 74, wherein the antiviral agent is remdesivir, favilavir, OYA1 , lopinavir, ritonavir, galidesivir, E1DD-1931 , EIDD-2801 , or SNG001 (inhaled interferon-beta-1 a). 76. The method of claim 73, wherein the one or more therapeutic agents comprise an antiparasitic agent. 77. The method of claim 76, wherein the antiparasitic agent is hydroxychloroquine or chloroquine. 78. The method of claim 73, wherein the one or more therapeutic agents comprise an antibacterial agent. 79. The method of claim 78, wherein the antibacterial agent is azithromycin. 80. The method of claim 73, wherein the one or more therapeutic agents comprise a second therapeutic antibody. 81. The method of claim 80, wherein the second therapeutic antibody is gimsilumab. 82. A method of detecting a betacoronavirus in a sample from a subject, the method comprising contacting the sample with the antibody of any one of claims 1 -40 under conditions permissive for binding of the antibody to a betacoronavirus and detecting whether a complex is formed between the antibody and the betacoronavirus. 83. The method of claim 82, wherein the sample is a swab sample, a lavage sample, a blood sample, a plasma sample, a sputum sample, a urine sample, a stool sample, or a mucosal secretion sample. 84. The method of claim 83, wherein the sample is a swab sample. 85. The method of claim 84, wherein the swab sample is a nasopharyngeal swab sample. 86. The method of claim 83, wherein the sample is a lavage sample. 87. The method of claim 86, wherein the lavage sample is a bronchoalveolar lavage sample. 88. The method of any one of claims 82-87, wherein the subject is presumed to have a betacoronavirus infection. 89. The method of any one of claims 54-88, wherein the subject is a mammal. 90. The method of claim 89, wherein the mammal is a human. 91. A kit comprising the antibody of any one of claims 1-40 and a package insert comprising instructions for using the antibody for treating a subject having or at risk of developing a disorder associated with a betacoronavirus infection. 92. A kit for detecting a betacoronavirus, the kit comprising the antibody of any one of claims 1-40 and a package insert comprising instructions for using the antibody to detect a betacoronavirus. 93. The kit of claim 92, wherein the antibody is conjugated to a label or a tag. 94. An antibody that binds SARS-CoV-2 S protein for treating a subject having a betacoronavirus infection or presumed to have a betacoronavirus infection, wherein the antibody: (a) binds to an epitope between amino acid residues 439-541 of SARS-CoV-2 S protein (SEQ ID NO: 1); or (b) comprises the following complementary determining regions (CDRs): (i) a CDR-H1 comprising the amino acid sequence of GFSFSSYGMH (SEQ ID NO: 2); (ii) a CDR-H2 comprising the amino acid sequence of WYDGSDK (SEQ ID NO: 3); (iii) a CDR-H3 comprising the amino acid sequence of ARERYFDWIFDF (SEQ ID NO: 4); (iv) a CDR-L1 comprising the amino acid sequence of RASQSVSSSYLA (SEQ ID NO: 5); (v) a CDR-L2 comprising the amino acid sequence of GASSRAT (SEQ ID NO:6); and (vi) a CDR-L3 comprising the amino acid sequence of QQYGSSWT (SEQ ID NO: 7). 95. An antibody that binds SARS-CoV-2 S protein for treating a subject at risk of having a betacoronavirus infection, wherein the antibody: (a) binds to an epitope between amino acid residues 439-541 of SARS-CoV-2 S protein (SEQ ID NO: 1); or (b) comprises the following complementary determining regions (CDRs): (i) a CDR-H1 comprising the amino acid sequence of GFSFSSYGMH (SEQ ID NO: 2); (ii) a CDR-H2 comprising the amino acid sequence of WYDGSDK (SEQ ID NO: 3); (iii) a CDR-H3 comprising the amino acid sequence of ARERYFDWIFDF (SEQ ID NO: 4); (iv) a CDR-L1 comprising the amino acid sequence of RASQSVSSSYLA (SEQ ID NO: 5); (v) a CDR-L2 comprising the amino acid sequence of GASSRAT (SEQ ID NO:6); and (vi) a CDR-L3 comprising the amino acid sequence of QQYGSSWT (SEQ ID NO: 7). 96. The antibody for use according to claim 94 and 95, wherein the antibody binds to an epitope between amino acid residues 439-498 of SARS-CoV-2 S protein (SEQ ID NO: 1). 97. The antibody for use according to any one of claims 94-96, wherein the antibody binds to an epitope comprising at least one of amino acid residues Y449, Y453, F456, A475, Y489, and Q493 of SARS-CoV-2 S protein (SEQ ID NO: 1). 98. The antibody for use according to claim 96, wherein the antibody binds to an epitope comprising the amino acid residues Y449, Y453, F456, A475, Y489, and Q493 of SARS-CoV-2 S protein (SEQ ID NO: 1). 99. The antibody for use according to any one of claims 94-98, wherein the betacoronavirus infection is with a lineage B betacoronavirus or a lineage C betacoronavirus. 100. The antibody for use according to claim 99, wherein the betacoronavirus infection is with a lineage B betacoronavirus. 101 . The antibody for use according to claim 100, wherein the lineage B betacoronavirus is SARS-CoV-2 or SARS-CoV. 102. The antibody for use according to claim 101 , wherein the lineage B betacoronavirus is SARS-CoV-2. 103. The antibody for use according to claim 101 , wherein the lineage B betacoronavirus is SARS-CoV. 104. The antibody for use according to any one of claims 94 and 99-102, wherein the wherein the subject has or is presumed to have coronavirus disease 19 (COVID-19). 105. The antibody for use according to any one of claims 94, 99-101 , and 103, wherein the subject has or is presumed to have severe acute respiratory syndrome (SARS). 106. The antibody for use according to any one of claims 94-105, wherein the antibody is formulated for administration to the subject at a dosage of about 0.1 mg/kg to about 100 mg/kg. 107. The antibody for use according to claim 106, wherein the antibody is formulated for administration to the subject at a dosage of about 1 mg/kg to about 80 mg/kg. 108. The antibody for use according to claim 107, wherein the antibody is formulated for administration to the subject at a dosage of about 1 mg/kg to about 40 mg/kg. 109. The antibody for use according to any one of claims 94-108, wherein the antibody is formulated for intravenous administration. 110. The antibody for use according to any one of claims 94-108, wherein the antibody is formulated for intranasal administration. 111. The antibody for use according to any one of claims 94-108, wherein the antibody is formulated for administration by inhalation. 112. The antibody for use according to any one of claims 94-111 , wherein the antibody is formulated for administration to the subject as a monotherapy. 113. The antibody for use according to any one of claims 94-111 , wherein the antibody is formulated for administration to the subject as a combination therapy. 114. The antibody for use according to claim 113, wherein the combination therapy further comprises one or more additional therapeutic agents that are formulated for administration to the subject. 115. The antibody for use according to claim 114, wherein the antibody is formulated for administration to the subject prior to, concurrently with, or after administration of the one or more additional therapeutic agents. 116. The antibody for use according to claim 114 or 115, wherein the one or more additional therapeutic agents comprise a second therapeutic antibody, an antifungal agent, an antiviral agent, an antiparasitic agent, an antibacterial agent, or a combination thereof. 117. The antibody for use according to claim 116, wherein the one or more additional therapeutic agents comprise an antiviral agent. 118. The antibody for use according to claim 117, wherein the antiviral agent is remdesivir, favilavir, OYA1 , lopinavir, ritonavir, galidesivir, EIDD-1931 , EIDD-2801 , or SNGOOI (inhaled interferon- beta- 1a). 119. The antibody for use according to claim 116, wherein the one or more therapeutic agents comprise an antiparasitic agent. 120. The antibody for use according to claim 119, wherein the antiparasitic agent is hydroxychloroquine or chloroquine. 121 . The antibody for use according to claim 116, wherein the one or more therapeutic agents comprise an antibacterial agent. 122. The antibody for use according to claim 121 , wherein the antibacterial agent is azithromycin. 123. The antibody for use according to claim 116, wherein the one or more therapeutic agents comprise a second therapeutic antibody. 124. The antibody for use according to claim 123, wherein the second therapeutic antibody is gimsilumab. 125. The antibody for use according to any one of claims 94-124, wherein the subject is a mammal. 126. The antibody for use according to claim 125, wherein the mammal is a human.
